title: delphinium scopulorum var. luporum
intro: Delphinium scopulorum var. luporum, also known as Lupine Larkspur or Mountain Lupine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. The plant contains delphinine, a toxic alkaloid that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits associated with Delphinium scopulorum var. luporum include relief from respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as potential antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made through infusion or tincture, and it is often used in traditional herbal remedies, although caution is advised due to its toxicity.
